user_message: Before my call with Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery, run the hygiene check. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 17:24 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 17:18 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 13:43 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-01-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery)

[00:01:15] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:08:00] David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:18:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:23:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - QBR preparation

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
